what_is_open: Determine the exact feasible welfare-regret exponent in the weak-overlap nuisance-limited regime gamma>0 with kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma): either prove an improved feasible estimator attaining r_star or prove a lower bound showing that feasible weak-arm nuisance learning forces a slower exponent.
obstruction: The solved feasible upper bound gives the explicit clipped-AIPW exponent r_feas=min{r_star,kappa zeta}. The minimax converse thm:minimax-lower applies to all measurable Pi-valued estimators and has exponent r_star, so it does not by itself prove that feasible procedures must be slower. Conversely, the clipped-AIPW proof contains the estimated-nuisance drift term r_mu r_e/q + r_mu q^{1/(2gamma)}, and the present core has no argument showing that this term is an artifact removable by another estimator.
attempted: The exact clipped-score identity was used with an oracle-clipping comparison to derive the best available bias-variance balance for the plain cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM. The two-point lower-bound construction was also checked, but it perturbs only the weak-arm outcome in a way that establishes the information benchmark r_star, not an additional feasible-nuisance lower bound tied to learning mu on clipped regions.
partial_result: If kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), including gamma=0, the feasible ERM attains r_star up to logarithms by oeq:feasible-upper and thm:minimax-lower, so tightness is resolved in that subregime. In the strict nuisance-limited subregime, the proved bracket is c n^{-r_star} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas}(log n)^p for the explicit feasible ERM, with r_feas<r_star; whether the upper exponent can be improved remains open.
